title: pteridium aquilinum
intro: Pteridium aquilinum, also known as the bracken fern, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the fern family and is native to the Northern Hemisphere. The plant contains a number of compounds with potential medicinal properties, including quercetin and kaempferol, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Consuming bracken fern can have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, but it should be used in moderation due to its potential toxicity. In herbal preparations, bracken fern is often used in teas and tinctures to support digestive health and reduce symptoms of menopause, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.
